_status: partially populated (2026-07-07 second + third research pass) - see data-requirements.md §17/§18 and ISSUES.md ISS-9/ISS-3

usefulLifeYears.note: Companies Act Schedule II explicitly names 'ultrasound machines' at 13 years, the diagnostic-scan category. See data-requirements.md §12.4/§14.

salvageValuePercentage.note: Companies Act Schedule II caps residual value at 5% of original cost unless separately justified. See data-requirements.md §18.2 for the citation-correction caveat (same as MRI).

installationAndAncillaryCostPercentage.note: No tender or study gives a stated percentage. A buying-guide site notes ultrasound site prep is light (power outlets, network, storage) vs. heavy imaging-equipment civil work -- this range is an inference from that description, not a measured figure. See data-requirements.md §18.3.

warrantyYears.note: AIIMS Patna tender: 5-year comprehensive warranty + 5yr CMC. Single tender, no corroborating source found -- kept at Medium confidence (not High) despite being the only figure available, per this doc's own confidence rules (High requires multiple sources to agree). See data-requirements.md §18.1. Normalized to {low,typical,high} 2026-07-13 (PBA-1) -- a single reported figure, not a real range, so low=typical=high.

cmcYears.note: Follows the 5-year warranty in the AIIMS Patna tender. See data-requirements.md §18.4. Normalized to {low,typical,high} 2026-07-13 (PBA-1) -- a single reported figure, not a real range, so low=typical=high.

amcAnnualCostPercentage.unit: percent of equipment cost per year
amcAnnualCostPercentage.note: Labour-only AMC. Generic tender clause (SCTIMST) applied identically across all 5 equipment types -- not Ultrasound-specific. See data-requirements.md §18.4.

cmcAnnualCostPercentage.unit: percent of equipment cost per year
cmcAnnualCostPercentage.note: Generic proxy (SCTIMST's tighter 3-5% CAMC cap, applied here rather than the broader 3-10% GeM range used for MRI/CT/Dialysis) -- not Ultrasound-specific research. See data-requirements.md §18.4.

_usagePerDayRange.note: No published Indian data exists. An unsourced international inference (15-30min/exam -> 16-32 scans/day) was explicitly flagged by the research pass as lacking an authoritative India source -- not used as a default.

billedTariffPerUse.note: Ultrasound whole-abdomen CGHS reimbursement ceiling -- a government-scheme floor, NOT a private cash tariff. Pelvis (~425/500) and small-parts (~655/770) studies price lower -- this field anchors to whole-abdomen as the baseline.

launchDelayMonths.note: Still an informal claim after a third pass, not a real timeline study: a buying-guide site says ultrasound units are usable almost immediately given minimal site needs. Converted here to ~0-2 weeks (0-0.5 month). See data-requirements.md §18.6.
